metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4948
metadata.comment:
Rationale: - K+-channel tetramerization domain-containing proteins (KCTDs) are auxiliary GABAB receptor (GBR) subunits.
- Experiments were performed at the synaptic connection from medial habenula (MHb) to interpeduncular nucleus (IPN).
-Using pre-embedding immunogold labeling, imaged with TEM, KCTD12 showed "particles for KCTD12 were found mostly on the postsynaptic side of asymmetrical synapses and only few synapses exhibited presynaptic KCTD12 labeling along synaptic and extrasynaptic membrane (Figure 3D, Figure 3 - figure supplement 2)"

- Figure 2B: immunolabeling of WT and KCTD12 KO mice shows antibody specificity. Experimental description: Wild-type C57BL/6J mice(Jax, Bar Harbor, ME; #000664) purchased from Jackson Laboratory.
BTB/POZ domain-containing protein KCTD12.
Antibody: rabbit anti-KCTD12 (1 ug/ul, 1 O/N, Bettler lab, Univ. Basel)

checks.label: OWL Consistency
checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
